methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $136M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
Cambridge Aerospace, a Series A defense & security company founded by Steven Barrett and Chris Sylvan, targets valuation using a peer set of 8 private companies at same/adjacent stage plus 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). The company has raised $136M to date and is currently fundraising at $200M.

Two triangulation methods apply here. Stage-progression analysis uses the $136M raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage advancement, implying $272M. Peer-median analysis across the 8-company Defense & Security comparable set yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range of $125M to $3.0B. Named peers include Dream Security ($3.0B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), and Castelion and Defense Unicorns (each $1.0B), all at Series B stage.

The triangulated analysis produces a low of $231M, median of $636M, and high of $1.1B. The wide dispersion between stage-progression and peer-median methods reflects material uncertainty around Cambridge Aerospace's growth trajectory relative to its peer cohort. Key variables driving valuation within this range include demonstrated revenue or customer traction, technological differentiation within defense applications, and whether the company's risk profile aligns with the lower or upper quartile of the peer distribution.
